notable_dependencies: Availability of openpyxl or Excel to verify and update XLSX
Access to the original packet to confirm opex cadence if possible
extra_model_fields.rollback_plan: If spreadsheet changes introduce inconsistencies, revert to baseline MACC_curve.xlsx and apply changes incrementally (first add RawInput tab/CSV, then modify formulas).
verification_checks_to_run_after_revisio: Open MACC_curve.xlsx with openpyxl and validate formulas programmatically.
Confirm RawInput.csv matches packet values exactly and that memo cell pointers resolve to correct cells.
Recompute net_abatement_cost_per_tco2e and ensure memo and metadata_json match.

planned_changes.description: Open MACC_curve.xlsx in an environment with openpyxl or Excel; confirm and, if necessary, fix all cell-level formulas so they match the memo formula and include year-by-year cash flows.
Add a 'RawInput' worksheet to MACC_curve.xlsx containing the exact packet inputs with explicit cell citations (e.g., 'input/task.json:12 -> RawInput!A2') and export that sheet to CSV (RawInput.csv).

Update memo.md to include: (a) an authoritative statement that the workbook RawInput tab (and RawInput.csv) is the reproduction source; (b) explicit pointers to key spreadsheet cells; (c) an alternate-results table if opex_savings are total-not-annual; and (d) a note about discounting and an input cell for discount rate in the spreadsheet.
Re-run internal validation checks and regenerate normalized metadata_json to reflect any changes (updated net_abatement_cost_per_tco2e values if cadence/discounting changed).
Add a short note in attempt metadata (final_message.txt or metadata_json) clarifying which components performed artifact-contract checks vs. semantic checks to remove internal inconsistency.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve workbook inspectability and reproducibility, clarify opex cadence and discounting assumptions, and add explicit cell-level references and a downloadable raw-input CSV so verifiers can validate formulas without environment constraints.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Improve workbook inspectability and reproducibility, clarify opex cadence and discounting assumptions, and add explicit cell-level references and a downloadable raw-input CSV so verifiers can validate formulas without environment constraints.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt produced the required artifacts (memo.md and MACC_curve.xlsx) with explicit packet citations, intermediate calculations, sensitivity checks, and a decision table. Full credit is justified. Two practical issues remain: (1) the workbook could not be opened in the verifier environment for formula-level inspection (openpyxl unavailable), so verification relied on the memo and normalized metadata rather than direct cell/formula checks; (2) the memo interprets opex savings as annual and documents this as 'needs_review' - confirm cadence and specify discounting if intended. Addressing these will improve reproducibility and reduce reviewer friction.
